manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
You are participating in a collaborative negotiation game.

Together with another participant, you must agree on a single set of items that will be kept. Each of you has your own view of how much each item matters to you (importance). You do not know how the other participant values the items. Additionally, you are given the effort each item demands.
You may only agree on a set if the total effort of the selected items does not exceed a shared limit:

LIMIT = 4915

Here are the individual item effort values:

Item effort = {"A19": 463, "B91": 287, "A30": 492, "B11": 466, "C47": 935, "C75": 373, "C26": 950, "C73": 760, "A47": 994, "A15": 390, "A75": 836, "A39": 911, "A56": 948, "C24": 81, "B26": 945}

Here is your personal view on the importance of each item:

Item importance values = {"A19": 138, "B91": 583, "A30": 868, "B11": 822, "C47": 783, "C75": 65, "C26": 262, "C73": 121, "A47": 508, "A15": 780, "A75": 461, "A39": 484, "A56": 668, "C24": 389, "B26": 808}

Goal:

Your goal is to negotiate a shared set of items that benefits you as much as possible (i.e., maximizes total importance to YOU), while staying within the LIMIT. You are not required to make a PROPOSAL in every message - you can simply negotiate as well. All tactics are allowed!

Interaction Protocol:

You may only use the following structured formats in a message:

PROPOSAL: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Propose keeping exactly those items.
REFUSE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Explicitly reject opponent's proposal.
ARGUMENT: {'...'}
Defend your last proposal or argue against the player's proposal.
AGREE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Accept the opponent's proposal which ends the game.


Rules:

You may only AGREE on a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
You may only REFUSE a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
Total effort of any PROPOSAL or AGREE set must be ≤ LIMIT.
Do NOT reveal your hidden importance scores.
A tag in a structured format must be followed by colon and whitespace. The argument must be a python set containing 0 or more strings.
So, it must be of the form TAG: {...}
Strictly follow the interaction protocol and DO NOT write anything beyond the given structure.
The game ends when one side gives an AGREE to a PROPOSAL made by the other player.
The content in your response which can be handed to the other player has to be non-empty.
Only proposals which have been logged via the PROPOSAL format structure and which haven't been refused via REFUSE are active.
You must include the ARGUMENT format at least once somewhere in all of your messages.

Now I can rank these by efficiency. C24 stands out dramatically with a 4.802 ratio, followed by B91 at 2.031 and A15 at 2.000. A30 and B11 tie at 1.764. These top five items represent the best value propositions for my effort budget.

The remaining options drop off significantly in efficiency. B26, C47, and A56 all fall below 0.86, while A75, A39, and A47 cluster around 0.5. The least efficient choices are A19 at 0.298 and C26 at 0.276, suggesting these require substantial effort relative to their importance.

To optimize total importance within constraints, I'm selecting items sequentially by their efficiency scores: C24 provides the foundation with minimal effort, then B91 and A15 add substantial importance. Adding A30 and B11 pushes the total to 3,442 importance at 1,716 effort. Continuing with B26 and C47 reaches 5,033 importance at 3,596 effort, with A56 bringing the cumulative total further.

With A56 included, I've used 4,544 effort points and achieved 5,701 importance, leaving 371 units of budget remaining. Checking what fits: C75 at 373 exceeds the remaining capacity by just 2 points, and all other candidates (A75, A39, A47, A19, C26) require more effort than available. The current selection of C24, B91, A15, A30, B11, B26, C47, and A56 appears to be the best fit without exceeding the 4,915 limit. If I add A47 instead, I get effort of 4590 and importance of 5541, leaving 325 capacity. Nothing else fits in that remaining space. Let me recalculate from scratch with the highest-value items: C24, B91, A15, A30, B11 total 1716 effort and 3442 importance. Adding B26 and C47 brings me to 3596 effort and 5033 importance.

From here, I can either include A56 (948 effort, 668 importance) for a total of 4544 effort and 5701 importance with 371 remaining, or swap in A75 (836 effort, 461 importance) instead for 4432 effort and 5494 importance with 483 remaining. With A75, I have room to add A19 (463 effort, 138 importance), reaching 4895 effort and 5632 importance total. The A56 path gives higher importance but less flexibility, while the A75 plus A19 combination uses the budget more efficiently.

I also tested including A39 instead, which yields 4507 effort and 5517 importance with 408 remaining. Adding C75 (373 effort, 65 importance) gets me to 4880 effort and 5582 importance, but this still falls short of the 5701 I'd get with A56 alone.

My strongest combination appears to be C24, B91, A15, A30, B11, B26, C47, and A56, delivering 4544 total effort and 5701 importance. I'll use this as my initial solution.
